display_name: ADC — Topoisomerase I inhibitor payload (SN-38 / DXd)
source: FDA 2024 ADC Guidance; T-DXd clinical trials; sacituzumab govitecan BLA review
description: Topoisomerase I inhibitors (SN-38, exatecan derivative DXd) cause DNA single-strand breaks leading to replication fork collapse. DXd is membrane-permeable with bystander effect. Primary DLT: neutropenia. CRITICAL: ILD (interstitial lung disease) occurs in 10-15% of T-DXd patients but is NOT reproduced in cynomolgus — a known preclinical-to-clinical translation gap.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: ANC <1.0 x 10^9/L = adverse. Severity correlates with free SN-38/DXd AUC.
expected_findings.rationale: Myeloid progenitor cytotoxicity from topoisomerase I inhibition.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Mucosal necrosis or hemorrhagic enteritis = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Intestinal crypt cell cytotoxicity. UGT1A1 polymorphism modulates sensitivity in humans.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: ALWAYS ADVERSE when present in preclinical models. ILD is a clinically emergent signal (~10-15% with T-DXd) not reproduced in cynomolgus. Preclinical absence does NOT rule out clinical risk.
expected_findings.rationale: ILD is NOT expected in preclinical models (typically absent in cyno). Any DAD or organizing pneumonia is always adverse. Immune-mediated component suspected.
expected_findings.translation_gap: ILD absent in cyno, 10-15% clinical incidence with T-DXd. Potentially fatal. Preclinical absence does NOT rule out risk.

expected_findings.rationale: Erythroid progenitor cytotoxicity from topoisomerase I inhibition.
Cycling follicular keratinocytes sensitive to topoisomerase I inhibition.
